result.stderr:
Usage: pxl-render <file.cs> [options]

Inputs:
  <file.cs>               C# pixogram script
  --stdin                 Read script from stdin

Outputs (repeat for multiple):
  --output <path>         File output; format inferred from extension:
                            .gif   → animated GIF
                            .apng  → animated PNG
                            .webp  → animated WebP
                            .pxl   → hex text frames
                            <dir>  → PNG frames directory
  --stdout                Stream raw RGB24 bytes to stdout
  --device <ip>           Stream to a PXL device at <ip> (port 5002)

Options:
  --duration <secs>       Duration to render in seconds (required)
  --fps <int>             Frames per second (default: 40)
  --width <int>           Canvas width in pixels (default: 24)
  --height <int>          Canvas height in pixels (default: 24)
  --scale <int>           Output scale factor for file outputs (default: 1)
  --mode <mode>           Render mode: raw | flat | clock (default: raw)
  --gap <float>           Pixel gap as fraction of cell size (default: 0 for raw, 0.05 for flat/clock)
  --timelapse <int>       Keep every Nth frame for file outputs (default: 1)
  --start-time <datetime> Virtual start time (default: current time)

Examples:
  pxl-render MyClock.cs --output preview.gif --duration 10
  pxl-render MyClock.cs --output preview.gif --output preview.webp --scale 10 --mode clock --duration 5
  pxl-render MyClock.cs --device 192.168.1.42 --duration 30
  pxl-render MyClock.cs --device 192.168.1.42 --output preview.gif --duration 10
  pxl-render MyClock.cs --stdout --duration 5 | ffmpeg -f rawvideo -pix_fmt rgb24 -s 24x24 -r 40 -i - output.mp4
